skill.name: sql-to-osc
skill.description: Converts Flyway SQL migration statements into Online Schema Change (OSC) text entries for project migration workflows.
skill.summary: Convert Flyway ALTER TABLE and CREATE INDEX migrations into OSC format.
skill.author: DennisLiuCk

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Ruby backtick command patterns and one weak cryptography pattern, but the cited lines are Markdown inline-code examples and YAML description text, not executable code. The skill does direct the assistant to read migration files and write an OSC output file, so it carries a low filesystem modification risk but no confirmed command execution, network, secret access, malware, or prompt injection behavior.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Output Guidance
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to read SQL migration files and write generated OSC output under src/main/resources/db/osc. This is expected for the workflow, but users should review generated migration artifacts before committing or deploying them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file read and output path are explicitly described in the workflow. The behavior is bounded to project migration paths and does not show destructive or hidden writes.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Markdown backtick examples as Ruby or shell execution. The cited content is documentation showing SQL syntax, output formatting, and example blocks, with no executable script or command runner present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text appears only inside Markdown documentation, tables, and fenced examples. No script file, shell invocation, eval, spawn, or subprocess API exists in the scanned skill.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged the frontmatter description line as a weak cryptographic algorithm. The cited line begins a natural language skill description and contains no cryptographic function or algorithm selection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is YAML frontmatter for the description field. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA1, cipher use, password hashing, or any cryptographic operation in the skill.

content.value_statement: Manual conversion of schema migrations to OSC files can introduce formatting errors. This skill applies a consistent OSC format for Flyway ALTER TABLE and CREATE INDEX migrations.
actual_capabilities: Extracts database and table names from Flyway SQL migration statements.
Converts ALTER TABLE operations into tab-separated OSC entries.
Transforms CREATE INDEX statements into ADD INDEX operations.
Normalizes NULL declarations to DEFAULT NULL where required.
Formats output with UTF-8, LF line endings, tabs, commas, and semicolons.
Provides a conversion summary with source file, output path, affected tables, and operation counts.
limitations: Focused on Flyway migration files that use USE, ALTER TABLE, and CREATE INDEX patterns.
Does not validate whether the generated OSC operation is safe for production databases.
Does not execute migrations or connect to a database server.
Complex SQL dialect features may require manual review after conversion.

use_cases.title: Prepare OSC Files for Backend Releases
use_cases.description: Convert planned Flyway schema changes into the OSC text format used by the release process.

use_cases.title: Review Migration Formatting Before Deployment
use_cases.description: Check that generated OSC entries use the required database, table, and operation columns.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Migration Output
use_cases.description: Apply the same OSC formatting rules across migration changes from multiple contributors.

prompt_templates.title: Convert One Migration
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner conversion for a single file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this Flyway SQL migration to OSC format. Show the output path and a short conversion summary.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Project Migration File
prompt_templates.scenario: Use the project migration directory and write the expected OSC file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the newest SQL migration in src/main/resources/db/migration and create the matching OSC output in src/main/resources/db/osc.

prompt_templates.title: Review an OSC Conversion
prompt_templates.scenario: Check generated output before committing it.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this OSC output against the source migration. Confirm database, table, operation order, separators, and semicolon placement.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Multiple Table Changes
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced conversion with several affected tables.
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert all ALTER TABLE and CREATE INDEX statements in this migration to OSC format. Group output by database and table, then summarize operation counts.

output_examples.input: A Flyway migration adds a nullable numeric column to one table and creates an index for that column.
output_examples.output: The result is one OSC line with database, table, and a comma-joined operation list. The summary names the source migration, output file, affected table, and operation counts.

output_examples.input: A reviewer provides an existing OSC text file and the original migration for comparison.
output_examples.output: The review identifies whether the table name, index conversion, DEFAULT NULL handling, tab separators, and final semicolon match the expected format.

output_examples.input: A migration changes several tables in the same database.
output_examples.output: The output contains separate OSC entries for each table, with operations ordered from the source migration and summarized by operation type.

best_practices: Review generated OSC files before committing them to version control.
Keep each migration source file available beside the generated OSC summary.
Validate production safety with your database release process before applying schema changes.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to execute database changes directly.
Do not skip review for migrations with complex SQL or multiple dialect features.
Do not mix unrelated database changes in one OSC output without clear table grouping.

faq.question: What does this skill convert?
faq.answer: It converts Flyway SQL migration statements into OSC text entries for online schema change workflows.

faq.question: Does it connect to my database?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides conversion guidance and file formatting. It does not open database connections.

faq.question: Which SQL statements are covered?
faq.answer: The documented workflow covers USE statements, ALTER TABLE operations, NULL handling, and CREATE INDEX conversion.

faq.question: Can it write files?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill instructs the assistant to write OSC output under src/main/resources/db/osc when used in a project workspace.

faq.question: Should I review the generated OSC file?
faq.answer: Yes. Review the file for table names, operation order, separators, and production safety before deployment.

faq.question: Is the static command execution warning confirmed?
faq.answer: No. The flagged backticks are Markdown formatting examples, not executable command calls.
